channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill
item.description:
State legislators are paving the way for children to overcome challenges with literacy, empowering them to thrive in their academic development and build flourishing lives for themselves and their families in the future.

The post Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Nebraska State Treasurer Joey Spellerberg. During the episode Treasurer Spellerberg and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss LB 938, which creates First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska, the role of the Treasurer’s Office, and solutions for Nebraska’s high property taxes. Listen now on Apple...

The post Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The Fraud that Wasn’t: Audit Shows Arizona ESA Program 98% Strong
item.description:
By Kathryn Hickok Recent news reporting in Arizona claimed that more than 20...

The post The Fraud that Wasn’t: Audit Shows Arizona ESA Program 98% Strong appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] How Alabama leaders are addressing social media and online safety
item.description:
Two lawsuits about the harms of social media ended with landmark rulings against social media giants such as Instagram, Facebook and YouTube.

The post How Alabama leaders are addressing social media and online safety appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] ExoticRidge v. Hawkins County: Fighting back against an arbitrary data center ban in East Tennessee
item.description: Executive Summary Technology is evolving like never before. Americans today benefit from rapid advancements in fields like artificial intelligence. These cloud-based advancements have a physical component: data centers. The data center industry has already generated trillions of dollars for the U.S. economy. And with companies projecting trillions of dollars more in investments to keep up...

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as host Jim Vokal rants about rising property taxes, the shell game elected officials are playing, and solutions Platte is advocating for this year. Listen now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

The post Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH’s rental housing is significantly older than its owner-occupied housing
item.description:
As America prepares to celebrate its 250th birthday, half of New Hampshire renters live in units built before the country celebrated its 200th birthday.  The median renter-occupied housing unit in New Hampshire was built in 1975, making it 51 years old this year, according to U.S. Census Bureau data.  By contrast, the median renter-occupied unit […]

The post NH’s rental housing is significantly older than its owner-occupied housing appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] What’s Really in the Governor’s Final Supplemental Budget Package
item.description:
Supplemental budgets are often framed as routine housekeeping, minor adjustments to keep state finances on track. The Governor’s 2026 supplemental, however, was anything but routine. It represented a troubling fiscal expansion that risked driving up costs for Maine families and taxpayers. The Governor did not stop there, however, and has since released a Change Package […]

The post What’s Really in the Governor’s Final Supplemental Budget Package appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 978 — Approp, H&W, pub hlth srvs, add’l (-2)
item.description: Note: This year IFF rated maintenance bills according to a more refined system. This is an enhancement bill, and will be rated as a standalone bill. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. Bill Description: House […]

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: The Final Days - and What Could Come Next
item.description:
We are officially in the final stretch of the legislative session. Sine Die — the last day of the regular session — is set for April 5. That means lawmakers have just days left to finalize remaining legislation, negotiate the state budget, and bring this year’s work to a close. But as things stand right […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: The Final Days - and What Could Come Next appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] TriMet’s $320M Painful Plan to Gridlock 82nd Avenue
item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Bus rapid transit was originally supposed to provide light-rail-like services...

The post TriMet’s $320M Painful Plan to Gridlock 82nd Avenue appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Albany Wavers on Shutting Down a Medicaid Racket
item.description:
As Washington threatens to crack down on fraud and abuse in New York’s Medicaid program, state legislators are doing their....

The post Albany Wavers on Shutting Down a Medicaid Racket appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 13
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 13 The 2026 legislative session is over… or is it? We break down the budget, education legislation, and a host of other bills that made it across the finish line during a busy week at the Capitol. 

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 13 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget
item.description:
Over the last several months, Maine Policy Institute has repeatedly pointed out the many problems that exist in Governor Mills’ 2026 supplemental budget. Maine lawmakers had an opportunity to fix serious flaws in the budget. Instead, the Legislature’s Appropriations Committee has taken an already problematic proposal from Janet Mills, doubled down, and made it worse. […]

The post From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Ranked Choice Voting the Wrong Approach
item.description:
The following appeared in the Albuquerque Journal on April 4, 2026 Albuquerque’s “progressives” are at it again. Ever since Republican RJ Berry won a three-way election for mayor in 2009 (against two Democrats), Albuquerque has had runoff elections requiring candidates to achieve more than 50% of the vote. These runoffs can be costly. The city […]

The post Opinion piece: Ranked Choice Voting the Wrong Approach appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant
item.description:
The following appeared on April 5, 2026 in the Santa Fe New Mexican. The article also appeared in papers across New Mexico. I recently came across a concept called the Curley Effect. It has nothing to do with Larry and Moe. After some research, I learned the Curley Effect, coined by economists Edward L. Glaeser […]

The post Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Empire Center Breaks Down Albany’s Pork Barrel Spending
item.description:
Albany legislators steered over $83 million in grants to 293 local projects between April and December 2025 , according to....

The post Empire Center Breaks Down Albany’s Pork Barrel Spending appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] A Rare Reform Opportunity in the Highway Bill
item.description:
Surface transportation re-authorization gives Congress a timely opportunity to confront lawsuit abuse and transportation affordability Congress is preparing...

The post A Rare Reform Opportunity in the Highway Bill appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Southern Resurgence - What the Numbers Tell Us
item.description:
Something significant is happening in America's South, and it deserves more attention. While New York and California are losing residents, states like South Carolina and Alabama are gaining population at a record pace - and alongside that growth, it is southern states like ours that are generating some of the most impressive economic numbers in the […]

The post The Southern Resurgence - What the Numbers Tell Us appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Foropportunity] GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act
item.description:
The Stronger Workforce for America Act of 2026 includes an invaluable option for state flexibility, the Make America Skilled Again Grants. Georgia and other states need this option so they can design workforce and safety net systems that meet families' immediate needs while providing a clearer path to opportunity and self-sufficiency.

The post GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] WASHINGTON: It’s about giving workers options
item.description:
Public sector unions are not just workplace representatives; they’re powerful political actors with influence that extends across ballot initiatives, elected officials, nonprofit organizations and super PACs. 

The post WASHINGTON: It’s about giving workers options appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Energy Reliability is Only as Strong as its Weakest Wire
item.description: The Volunteer State is building a serious energy future that will continue to anchor our leadership in the sector. Allowing competition and technology to improve our state's grid will not only make the state more attractive for investment but also keep more Tennesseans safe when it matters most.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] AEA leaves members in the dark as Alabama Policy Institute, Freedom Foundation weigh in on payroll deduction ban bill
item.description:
Every week during the legislative session, the Alabama Education Association (AEA) sends its members The Capitol Pulse, highlighting its priorities and informing them about pending, filed and moving legislation impacting teachers.

The post AEA leaves members in the dark as Alabama Policy Institute, Freedom Foundation weigh in on payroll deduction ban bill appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome William Beard, co-author of the Goldwater Institute’s report: Data Centers a Free Market Model for the Digital Future. During the episode William and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss what data centers are, why they matter, and what separates the states that are winning investment...

The post Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution
item.description:
The Maine Supreme Judicial Court has issued a unanimous advisory opinion in which the Court concluded that the Legislature’s attempt to expand ranked-choice voting (RCV) to general elections for governor, state senator, and state representative would violate the Maine Constitution.  Although the decision came quickly, the outcome closely tracks the concerns that surfaced during oral […]

The post Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] CALIFORNIA: Union hubris in any language
item.description:
After Freedom Foundation’s National Canvassing Director Dustin James reported that Spanish-speaking union represented workers were being taken advantage of, I knew I had to go down and see it for myself.

The post CALIFORNIA: Union hubris in any language appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Idaho’s Budget Grew 2.5% Despite the Cuts, because of Welfare Spending
item.description: If you were paying attention to the budget debates on both the House and Senate floors during the legislative session, you might think the Legislature made significant cuts to the Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27) budget. Unfortunately, based on preliminary numbers, total appropriations are a 2.5% increase, with FY27’s $14.453 billion outpacing the FY26 original appropriation […]

item.title: [Jbartlett] SNAP candy & soft drink ban would hurt retailers
item.description:
Removing candy and soft drinks from New Hampshire’s SNAP program would cost retailers an estimated $5.3 million-$17.8 million a year in lost candy and soft drink sales alone, a new study by the Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy finds. The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services and the U.S. Department of Agriculture have […]

The post SNAP candy & soft drink ban would hurt retailers appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Albany Should Listen to Jamie Dimon
item.description:
In his annual message to shareholders, JP Morgan Chase’s chief executive, Jamie Dimon, offered a timely and pointed warning for....

The post Albany Should Listen to Jamie Dimon appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] How a Levy Cap Works and Why Florida Needs One
item.description:
Florida property taxes now exceed $55 billion per year — the single largest source of local government revenue....

The post How a Levy Cap Works and Why Florida Needs One appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Don’t Tax the World Cup, How New Jersey Could Get This Wrong
item.description:
By Wells Winegar New Jersey is about to host one of the biggest global events in the world. The FIFA […]

The post Don’t Tax the World Cup, How New Jersey Could Get This Wrong first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Wisconsin’s southern border shows what freedom brings
item.description:
From 2011 to 2024, Wisconsin counties beat their Illinois counterparts 103 percent to 68 percent in private-sector economic output.

The post Wisconsin’s southern border shows what freedom brings appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Growing Louisiana’s Workforce: Why HB 680 Is Essential to “One Door to Work”
item.description:
Louisiana stands at a pivotal moment. With tens of billions of dollars in new economic development projects on the horizon and major policy changes like Medicaid work requirements set to increase workforce participation, the state’s ability to connect people to jobs has never been more important. Yet Louisiana’s current workforce system—fragmented, bureaucratic, and slow-moving—was not...

The post Growing Louisiana’s Workforce: Why HB 680 Is Essential to “One Door to Work” appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Legislative Session Wrap Up: Progress, Setbacks, and What’s Next
item.description:
The 2026 legislative session has concluded. Lawmakers could return to address final items, such as veto overrides, but the session is effectively over. As we reflect on the last few months, one word comes to mind to describe this legislative session: disappointing. We will likely remember this session more for what didn’t get done than…

The post 2026 Legislative Session Wrap Up: Progress, Setbacks, and What’s Next first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] A Step in the Right Direction on Energy, Now Let’s Finish the Job
item.description:
by Wells Winegar New Jersey just made a long overdue move. With Governor Mikie Sherrill signing legislation to lift the […]

The post A Step in the Right Direction on Energy, Now Let’s Finish the Job first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Entergy’s New Mega-Deal: How Does It Measure Up to President Trump’s Ratepayer Protection Pledge?
item.description:
Entergy Louisiana’s March 27 announcement of a dramatically expanded power agreement with Meta for the Hyperion data-center campus in Richland Parish marks one of the largest single commitments of private capital in Louisiana history. What began as a $10 billion project in December 2024 has ballooned to roughly $27 billion. Meta is adding 1,400 acres...

The post Entergy’s New Mega-Deal: How Does It Measure Up to President Trump’s Ratepayer Protection Pledge? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Week in Review: Civics Not DEI
item.description:
Every university student deserves a robust American civics education—in Arizona it’s not a suggestion, it’s a requirement. But a new..

The post Week in Review: Civics Not DEI appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. The Oregon Department of Energy and the Oregon...

The post John Charles Submits Public Comments on ODOE’s “TIGHGER 2.0” Climate Gap Measures appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Andrew Fowler Featured in The Wall Street Journal
item.description:
Communications Specialist Andrew Fowler points out in The Wall Street Journal how New England governors are acknowledging renewables alone can’t meet rising energy demands, arguing that expanding nuclear power alongside […]

The post Andrew Fowler Featured in The Wall Street Journal appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Federal ROAD to Housing Act Passes Senate, Generates Controversy Over Investor Limitations
item.description:
This April edition of The House Call discusses a federal bill that, if passed, would be the most comprehensive supply-focused federal housing legislation enacted in decades. It also explains Massachusetts’ starter home zoning district program and its potential implementation challenges.

The post Federal ROAD to Housing Act Passes Senate, Generates Controversy Over Investor Limitations appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 62 Representatives rejecting a generic assessment limit say “tough noogies” to people being taxed out of their homes
item.description: The alleged difference in the constitutional assessment limit between the House and the Senate was that the Senate favored a fixed rate (as do the vast majority of voters), while the House favored a rolling average of assessed values. I say ‘alleged’ because it didn’t appear that the House Tax Committee and others wanted a […]

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] How building new homes benefits everyone
item.description:
A new report from economists at the University of Hawai‘i makes a point that many of us have known all along: If we want to end the housing crisis, we need to build more homes. That’s because each new unit creates a chain of housing openings. A family that moves into a new house leaves […]

The post How building new homes benefits everyone appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] The Data Center Debate: Fact vs. Fiction
item.description:
The digital economy runs on data centers, a vital core of modern infrastructure most people never see. But there’s a..

The post The Data Center Debate: Fact vs. Fiction appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Idaho ends taxpayer support for teachers’ unions
item.description:
Idaho Gov. Brad Little on April 10 signed into law a Freedom Foundation-supported measure crafted to end taxpayer support for teachers' unions in the Gem State. 

The post Idaho ends taxpayer support for teachers’ unions appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Bethlehem Manor LLC v. City of Bethlehem: Fighting back against immunity for arbitrary zoning decisions at the Supreme Court
item.description: Beacon is fighting back against immunity for arbitrary zoning decisions at the Supreme Court.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Eichenberger: A Bubble-Wrapped Childhood
item.description: by Dan Eichenberger, MD, MBA In the past 15 years, rates of anxiety, depression, self-harm and suicidal thoughts among adolescents — especially those born after 1995 — have surged. Social psychologist Jonathan Haidt calls this the “Great Rewiring” of childhood: the decline of play-based childhoods and the rise of phone-based ones. The uncomfortable truth is that well-meaning Read the full article...

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Represents Coalition of Parents, Taxpayers, and Schools to Protect Choice Program and Taxpayers
item.description:
WILL represents a group of Wisconsin taxpayers, parents, and choice schools in a motion to intervene in Wisconsin PTA v. Wisconsin State Assembly. The complaint in that case seeks to have the judiciary determine the level and distribution of funding for education in this State rather than leave those decisions to the Legislature and seeks to reduce or eliminate Wisconsin’s School Choice Programs.

The post WILL Represents Coalition of Parents, Taxpayers, and Schools to Protect Choice Program and Taxpayers appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansans Need a Responsible Budget
item.description: The Kansas House and Senate have already passed the FY 2027 budget bill, House Bill 2513, and sent it to Governor Laura Kelly. Aside from some line-item vetoes and (failed) overrides, the topline is essentially the same. Supporters are calling it responsible because it trims the State General Fund budget by $189.2 million below a […]

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Senate Passes Property Tax Reform
item.description:
Legislators have taken the first step. Last week, the Iowa Senate approved an amended property tax reform bill on a vote of 41–4. The House Republicans’ version was passed by the House Ways and Means Committee on March 18 and is expected to move forward in the next few weeks. These proposals share common goals but take different approaches, including caps on local government revenue growth, changes to assessment practices, shifts in school funding, limits on TIF, and varying mixes of exemptions, freezes, and tax base adjustments. Governor Reynolds and legislative leaders from both parties described this as the first step in negotiations. Their goal is to reach a final property tax reform agreement this year.

The post Senate Passes Property Tax Reform appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home
item.description:
U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home In a time of growing global instability, the role of American strength is back at the center of the conversation. In this episode, Grant sits down with Roger Wicker, Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee and Mississippi’s senior senator. From rebuilding America’s military strength to…

The post U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Victory! Kansas Ends Mandatory DEI in Higher Ed
item.description:
Lawmakers in Kansas struck a blow against the discriminatory ideology of “diversity, equity, and inclusion” (DEI) by ending required DEI indoctrination..

The post Victory! Kansas Ends Mandatory DEI in Higher Ed appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WI Regulation Violates New SCOTUS Precedent Regarding Talk Therapy
item.description:
Wisconsin Family Action, and WILL’s client Joy Buchman are warning Governor Tony Evers and his Administration to immediately cease any enforcement of Wis. Admin. Code. § MPSW 20.02(25), a rule banning consensual, client-driven talk therapy from a perspective the government disfavors. The rule constitutes viewpoint discrimination, similar to the Colorado law recently struck down in Chiles v. Salazar.

The post WI Regulation Violates New SCOTUS Precedent Regarding Talk Therapy appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] March mass layoffs hit Cook County hardest
item.description:
Nearly 1,100 cuts were reported statewide in a range of industries.

The post March mass layoffs hit Cook County hardest appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Missouri Takes a First Step on Classroom Screen Time
item.description:
Listen to this article The Missouri House of Representatives recently passed House Bill (HB) 2230, a bill focused on limiting screen time for students in public schools. This legislation is […]

The post Missouri Takes a First Step on Classroom Screen Time appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy
item.description:
by Megan Whitman, NJPI Jr. Fellow Walk down any main street in New Jersey and you’re looking at the heart […]

The post Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Your Property Tax Bill in 2030
item.description:
By 2030, many Wisconsin taxpayers could face thousands in new taxes, with costs falling heaviest on local taxpayers even under the most optimistic assumptions about new state aid. The interactive calculator we released makes these impacts concrete. Policymakers and voters should carefully weigh whether this path truly serves students and communities or simply expands government at the expense of hardworking Wisconsin families. 

The post Your Property Tax Bill in 2030 appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Mackinac] What election reforms are actually possible?
item.description: Reforms should build confidence no matter who wins

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Cost of California’s High-Speed Rail Goes Up Again
item.description:
It was also supposed to be carrying 65.5 million to 96.5 million intercity riders a year by 2030. Yet now 2040 is the date for “full service to start.” Skeptics don’t believe we’ll ever see the train run with paying customers aboard. “In my judgment, the Draft 2026 Business Plan describes a project that has reached a dead end,” says Louis S. […]

The post Cost of California’s High-Speed Rail Goes Up Again appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] What Trump Can Learn From Reagan About Blockading The Strait Of Hormuz
item.description:
History’s winning formula is clear: Reject proportionality, embrace decisive force, and see the mullahs’ grip weaken. As the United States Navy enforces a full blockade of Iranian ports and conducts minesweeping operations in the Strait of Hormuz, the lessons of 1987 and 1988 are once again proving their worth. I know, because I participated in the Pentagon...

The post What Trump Can Learn From Reagan About Blockading The Strait Of Hormuz first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Unstacking Public Education IF YOU DIDN’T already think it a bad idea to allow public-sector workers to hire their own bosses and set their own salaries in a self-serving fiscal doom loop then the headline this week in the Capital Chronicle should have been eye-opening: “Union for ISTA Staff Files Unfair Labor Charges.”  Yes, the teachers union’s own employees Read the full article...

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Uncle Sam Needs a Bigger Boat
item.description:
Federal deficit spending has reached unsustainable levels, with the national debt surpassing $39 trillion, driven by decades of expanding government involvement in the economy. Even small, well-intentioned programs, like federal grants to support young commercial fishermen, contribute to a broader pattern where nearly every challenge is met with new spending. When government spending replaces the support of family, community, and the free market, we are pushed closer and closer to a fiscal crisis.

The post Uncle Sam Needs a Bigger Boat appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut’s Tax System Isn’t Broken — It’s Designed This Way
item.description:
On Tax Day, most Americans focus on a simple question: how much they owe.  In Connecticut, a different question looms just beneath the surface: why so much, and why hasn’t […]

The post Connecticut’s Tax System Isn’t Broken — It’s Designed This Way  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Washington fails the test for affordability
item.description: Washington continues to rack up poor rankings as one of the most unaffordable states in the country. A recent report shows the Evergreen State as the 5th most expensive state in the United States.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Swalwell Scandal Rocks Governor’s Race
item.description:
This week, Tim and Matt discuss the sudden departure from the governor’s race of disgraced and now former Congressman Eric Swalwell following sexual assault allegations. They discuss why incidents of bad behavior and other rumors that are common knowledge in political and government circles often don’t get media coverage. They analyze the impact of his […]

The post Swalwell Scandal Rocks Governor’s Race appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Housed in Dignity
item.description:
I recently learned the story of a 70-year-old woman who had spent her entire life in public housing. She was one of several generations in her family to live entirely in government housing. When someone can spend a lifetime in the same poverty-based system, “compassion” begins to look less like true help and more like...

The post Housed in Dignity first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] What The Atlantic Gets Right — And Wrong — About The Mississippi ‘Miracle’
item.description:
The highly publicized “Mississippi Miracle” — the implementation of teacher training, literacy coaches, and grade retention for struggling students that skyrocketed the state’s ranking from 49th in 2013 to nineth in 2024 — has created a well-earned outpouring of praise. Here at the Texas Public Policy Foundation, we have taken notice. In fact, the Lone...

The post What The Atlantic Gets Right — And Wrong — About The Mississippi ‘Miracle’ first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Mass refugees have brought $6 billion in personal income to NH since 2012
item.description:
Movers from Massachusetts to New Hampshire brought with them $5.95 billion in net adjusted gross income (AGI) from 2012-2023, IRS data show, representing a massive transfer of wealth from the high-tax Bay State to low-tax New Hampshire.  New Hampshire’s net personal income gain from Massachusetts movers grew tenfold from 2012-2019, then suddenly doubled to a […]

The post Mass refugees have brought $6 billion in personal income to NH since 2012 appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Meghan Portfolio Featured in The Washington Post
item.description:
Yankee Institute Manager of Research and Analysis Meghan Portfolio discusses in The Washington Post how a proposed regulation on self-checkouts could lead to rising costs for stores and limited services for shoppers. […]

The post Meghan Portfolio Featured in The Washington Post appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] New Study Calls for Reducing or Eliminating Parking Requirements for New Housing
item.description:
Data show the requirements increase rents, reduce housing development 

The post New Study Calls for Reducing or Eliminating Parking Requirements for New Housing appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

The Alabama Policy Institute on Monday declared the 2026 regular session its most productive on record.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] When Diversity Becomes Discrimination
item.description:
Listen to this article The U.S. Department of Justice has joined a lawsuit alleging race and sex discrimination against the Missouri State High School Activities Association (“MSHSAA”), and rightfully so, […]

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Rocklin Unified Takes Parental Notification Fight to U.S. Supreme Court
item.description:
Rocklin Unified School District (RUSD) filed a petition for a writ of certiorari with the U.S. Supreme Court on Tuesday in a case challenging the authority of California’s labor relations board to override the constitutional rights of parents. In the petition filed on behalf of RUSD by California Justice Center and the Liberty Justice Center,...

The post Rocklin Unified Takes Parental Notification Fight to U.S. Supreme Court first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Pelican Institute Urges Support for SB 490: Protecting Louisiana Families from Rising Energy Costs While Powering Economic Growth
item.description:
As Louisiana lawmakers and utility regulators convene today to address growing electricity demand and rising utility bills, the Pelican Institute for Public Policy is expressing strong support for Senate Bill 490.  In a letter sent today to Senate President Cameron Henry, Senate Commerce Committee Chairwoman Beth Mizell, and committee members, Pelican urges lawmakers to advance...

The post Pelican Institute Urges Support for SB 490: Protecting Louisiana Families from Rising Energy Costs While Powering Economic Growth appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] MOScholars Wins in Court
item.description:
Listen to this article Missouri’s education savings account program, MOScholars, which facilitates school choice in Missouri with student scholarships, got a decisive win in court this week. This is not […]

The post MOScholars Wins in Court appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Can California Thrive on Renewable Electricity?
item.description:
California’s state government has set an official goal of “net zero” by 2045. That’s less than 19 years from now. Already in pursuit of that goal, the state has managed to have the highest priced gasoline and the highest priced electricity in the entire continental United States. Condemning its residents to a poverty inducing bleeding...

The post Can California Thrive on Renewable Electricity? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Tax Day: A Reflection on State Income Taxes and Competitiveness
item.description:
April 15th is more than a filing deadline – it’s a moment to reflect on how tax policy shapes the economic trajectory of Massachusetts. As residents finalize their returns, a broader question looms: are current tax policies helping the state grow, or quietly pushing people and capital...

The post Tax Day: A Reflection on State Income Taxes and Competitiveness  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Watts: Book Review
item.description: ‘Taxes Have Consequences: An Income Tax History of the United States’ by Arthur Laffer, Brian Domitrovic and Jeanne Cairns by Tyler Watts, Ph.D. “Taxes Have Consequences” (THC) is a masterful survey of income taxation in the United  States since the ratification of the 16th amendment in 1913. The authors make a com pelling case that Read the full article...

item.title: [Johnlocke] Database proves claims about NYT bias
item.description:
Ira Stoll writes for the Washington Free Beacon about new data exposing a major media outlet’s journalistic malpractice. A new database of all the New York Times articles published in the past 25 years provides empirical evidence of some of the biases that many of the newspaper’s critics have long suspected. The developer of the...

The post Database proves claims about NYT bias appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Exposing another ‘disastrous’ California rail project
item.description:
Chris Bray writes for the Federalist about another Golden State boondoggle. In a much-discussed phenomenon, blue state government spending is soaring, and doing so against limited population growth. … … The culture of progressive governance lights money on fire for symbolic performances that never produce the promised results, and it creates money-hungry projects that become...

The post Exposing another ‘disastrous’ California rail project appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Kitchen-table issues could help GOP control Senate
item.description:
Ward Clark writes for RedState.com about interesting new polling data. In recent decades, it has become an accepted feature of American national politics that the party in power will lose seats in both the House of Representatives and the Senate in mid-term elections. There are many reasons for this, not the least of which is...

The post Kitchen-table issues could help GOP control Senate appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

The state’s unemployment rate is near the top nationwide

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Polls are clear: Voters want donated education money
item.description:
A federal scholarship program is popular among voters in Illinois and nationwide. Gov. J.B. Pritzker should opt in so Illinois students aren’t left behind.

item.title: [Mspolicy] AI & the Liberty Movement
item.description:
Amazon has just announced another multi-billion-dollar data center project in Mississippi — the latest in a flood of inward investment now pouring into our state. But here’s the thing worth reflecting on: even AI-related investments on this scale are only a fraction of what is flowing into data centers and AI infrastructure across the country. […]

The post AI & the Liberty Movement  appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mackinac] On housing, local vs. state control is the wrong question
item.description: Who controls matters less than how much

item.title: [Itrfoundation] New Graduation Rates Are a Starting Point, Not the Final Answer
item.description:
Graduation rates are a useful, but incomplete, measure: They show whether students are finishing school and provide public accountability, but they don’t fully capture academic readiness or learning outcomes. Iowa results are mixed: From 2024–2025, graduation rates rose slightly statewide (+0.5%) with more districts increasing than decreasing, but over five years, rates declined overall (-1.4%). Context matters when interpreting changes: Large swings, especially in smaller districts, can reflect small student count changes, and gaps between graduation rates and academic performance may warrant closer scrutiny.

The post New Graduation Rates Are a Starting Point, Not the Final Answer appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description:
The Law Centre today delivered a formal letter to Dr. Jerome Larkin, Director of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH), demanding that the agency immediately cease enforcement of Rhode Island’s ban on conversion therapy for minors. The action follows the U.S. Supreme Court’s March 21, 2026 decision in Chiles v. Salazar, which struck down Colorado’s similar law as a violation of the First Amendment.

The post Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] BOOK REVIEW: Does backing the blue mean backing unions’ ‘Blue Power’?
item.description:
Five years ago, in response to George Floyd’s death, the Brookings Institution and the American Enterprise Institute issued a report outlining methods to move toward a “criminal justice system — police, courts, prison, reentry, community supervision — that is focused on the safety, health and well-being of communities rather than on maintaining a harsh, semi-militarized […]

The post BOOK REVIEW: Does backing the blue mean backing unions’ ‘Blue Power’? appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] In 2025, state government spending per person was higher in Minnesota than in 45 other states
item.description:
On Tuesday, I noted that for 2025, the average earner will pay more state income tax in Minnesota than in 43 other states and that our state is one of just 16 where the share of the average earner’s wages swallowed up by the state government in income tax has increased over the last decade....

The post In 2025, state government spending per person was higher in Minnesota than in 45 other states appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Idaho refines Parental Choice Tax Credit after strong first-year launch
item.description: Idaho’s Parental Choice Tax Credit launched with strong momentum, supported by careful and deliberate planning by the legislature. From the outset, policymakers were intentional about designing a program that would be both accessible to families and administratively workable.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] HCMC experiencing classic case of ‘Go Woke, Go Broke’
item.description:
The hospital conducted 386 DEI coaching sessions across 61 departments. It delivered mandatory LGBTQ+ education on topics such as "pronouns, deadnaming, trans identities, and allyship."

The post HCMC experiencing classic case of ‘Go Woke, Go Broke’ appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Measure what matters: family structure and its impact on learning
item.description:
In 1969, nearly nine out of 10 American children lived in households with two married parents. By 2023, that figure had fallen to roughly six in 10.

The post Measure what matters: family structure and its impact on learning appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Habeas workaround
item.description:
Another Minnesota federal district judge flouts appeals court ruling. In two habeas corpus cases this week, Judge Donavon Frank (Clinton appointee) has ruled in favor of two illegal alien ICE detainees, in apparent defiance of an 8th circuit court of appeals decision issued on March 25. Last month, the 8th circuit overruled a different Minnesota-based...

The post Habeas workaround appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Kneecapping Conservatives, Protecting Bureaucrats: Vander Woude’s Lame Duck Legacy
item.description: As the 2026 legislative session reached its disappointing end, so too did Chairman John Vander Woude’s tenure in the Legislature. Rather than attempting to solidify his legacy as one of defense for children, families, and freedom, he seemingly spent his final days in the Capitol running interference for the Idaho Department of Health and Welfare, […]

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 2026 Green Book
item.description: 2026 Kansas Green Book – Exploring the relationship between the size of government and economic growth

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Analysis: Idaho tax cuts led to increase in state tax revenue
item.description: Over the past decade, Idaho lawmakers steadily reduced the state’s top income tax rate—from 7.4% in 2014 to a flat 5.3% today. Critics warned of budget shortfalls, underfunded services, and fiscal instability. Instead, Idaho is collecting more revenue than ever.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Referendums on development could kill state’s growth
item.description:
Subjecting big development proposals to popular vote risks killing statewide economic growth, observers say in the wake of a successful effort by Port Washington data center opponents to give citizens the ability to nix the future use of a key financing tool.

The post Referendums on development could kill state’s growth appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] DPI Dismisses Questions on Testing Changes, Waterpark Weekend
item.description:
DPI Dismisses Questions on Testing Changes, Waterpark Weekend The Department of Public Instruction’s legislative liaison Andrew Hoyer-Booth began a Wednesday hearing in front of the Assembly’s Committee on Government Operations, Accountability, and Transparency by telling lawmakers they wouldn’t get many answers. Wisconsin’s public school managers took a defiant tone with state lawmakers who have questions […]

The post DPI Dismisses Questions on Testing Changes, Waterpark Weekend appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Republican lawmakers say Wisconsin DPI violated open meetings law with Dells trip
item.description:
Republican lawmakers say Wisconsin DPI violated open meetings law with Dells trip DPI says education session included teachers, private vendor per state statute By Corrinne Hess April 15, 2026 New testing standards were rolled out for Wisconsin students in 2024. Shortly after, educators from private, public and charter schools across the state met in the Wisconsin Dells for a […]

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] No, You Can’t Legislate Morality or Abundance
item.description:
If you listen to the buzz in progressive state capitols and city halls across the country, or read Ezra Klein’s Abundance, you’d think our biggest problem is a crisis of state capacity. Klein, and a growing number of progressives, share a vision of a liberalism that builds. They argue that the government has become proficient...

The post No, You Can’t Legislate Morality or Abundance first appeared on California Policy Center.
